mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal manufacturing bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, triggering short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.3 percentage points triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb CPI spikes without negative 20-day returns, showing a positive return rate above 60% following threshold breaches.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor improvement signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 55% across all samples.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production falls while real GDP remains flat indicates supply-side degradation that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative monthly equity returns during economic stagnation.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence pattern does not precede negative 20-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 60% of triggered instances.
